title: Natural Religion
content:
Up through the mystic deeps of sunny air
    I cried to God - 'O Father, art Thou there?'
    Sudden the answer, like a flute, I heard:
    It was an angel, though it seemed a bird.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Richard Le Gallienne, titled "Natural Religion", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
